page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 372 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across various workloads. The CPU achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a multi-core score of 17983.0. In data processing tasks, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.54 GB/sec and image processing. It also shows strong capabilities in cryptographic operations, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 46227.0, and high-throughput database operations, executing 58.32 million Redis SET operations per second.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning. These hardware accelerators enable strong performance in small-to-medium LLM inference tasks, achieving 208.07 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B model. However, for larger models like the 70B parameter variant, text generation performance drops to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-48 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, enterprise application hosting, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 372 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance exhibits strong performance in single-core benchmarks, data decompression, and cryptographic operations, alongside top-tier image and text processing capabilities. Supported by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, it delivers strong performance in small-to-medium LLM inference and Redis database operations. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, software compilation, and enterprise application hosting, offering a balanced, high-density memory profile without the overhead of local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 372 GB of RAM, and advanced AMX instruction support for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized, high-density compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions for accelerated data processing and enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM (7.75 GB per core)
Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 260 MB L3 cache
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Strong performance in Redis operations and single-threaded decompression
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for in-memory databases, enterprise apps, and software compilation
